nodes.label: AI as time-saving documentation tool
nodes.grounding_span: It saves me SO MUCH TIME. Before it took me 30 minutes to complete one, now it's not even 5.

nodes.label: AI accuracy and truthfulness
nodes.grounding_span: I do check it and read it thoroughly to make sure that it is accurate and that there are no hallucinations

nodes.label: AI-generated text authenticity
nodes.grounding_span: I like it to use my style of writing so that my work doesn't *look* AI generated

nodes.label: Openness about AI use at work
nodes.label_negative: Stigma and secrecy around AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I don't tell my colleagues that I use it

nodes.label: AI as enhancer of output quantity and quality
nodes.label_negative: AI as threat to personal skill and authenticity
nodes.grounding_span: parents are getting MORE observations of their children because my output has increased

nodes.label: AI reliance and skill atrophy
nodes.grounding_span: I do worry that using it too often for this kind of work will slow my brain down a little

nodes.label: Work efficiency and reduced cognitive load
nodes.grounding_span: it's a relief when I can just use AI to do that kind of hard work for me when my brain is already fried

nodes.label: Professional credibility and accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: accidentally providing a parent a report of fake news just wouldn't be ok

nodes.label: Personal authenticity and skill preservation
nodes.grounding_span: I'm worried that my own observations and reports just would not be up to par

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for documentation
nodes.grounding_span: I am more satisfied than frustrated

nodes.label: Frustration with AI hallucinations
nodes.grounding_span: It can be quite frustrating again when it adds details that are embellished or just not true

nodes.label: Concern about skill atrophy
nodes.grounding_span: I do worry that using it too often for this kind of work will slow my brain down a little

nodes.label: Ambivalence about AI inevitability in education
nodes.grounding_span: the reality is that we're now starting to be overloaded with paperwork that it seems like using AI might be inevitable

nodes.label: Proofreading and iterative correction
nodes.grounding_span: I catch them by proof reading the AI outputs instead of blindly copy and pasting

nodes.label: Template-based structured delegation
nodes.grounding_span: I have fed an AI examples of my own personal observations as an example template
